mission.title: Install and Configure Milvus
mission.description: Milvus is a high-performance, cloud-native vector database designed for scalable vector approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search. It is ideal for applications that require efficient organization and searching of vast amounts of unstructured data. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes for deployment and the complexity of managing distributed systems.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Milvus CLI, which is necessary for managing your Milvus deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/releases/download/v2.5.0/milvus-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf milvus-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv milvus-cli /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Milvus CLI tarball.
> Extract the CLI binary.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kubernetes cluster on GKE with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create milvus-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.30

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local environment to use the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials milvus-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> Fetch the cluster credentials.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Install Milvus
steps.description:
This step installs Milvus using Helm, which simplifies the deployment process.

```bash

helm repo add milvus https://milvus-io.github.io/milvus-helm

helm install milvus milvus/milvus --namespace milvus --create-namespace --version 2.5.0

```

> Add the Milvus Helm repository.
> Install Milvus in the 'milvus' namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Milvus deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/milvus-io/milvus/master/deploy/monitoring/monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/milvus-io/milvus/master/deploy/autoscaling/autoscaling.yaml

```

> Apply monitoring configurations.
> Apply autoscaling configurations.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Milvus
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Milvus deployment and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall milvus --namespace milvus

kubectl delete namespace milvus

```

> Uninstall the Milvus Helm release.
> Delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Milvus
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step upgrades Milvus to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade milvus milvus/milvus --namespace milvus --version 2.6.11

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods -n milvus

```

> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrade Milvus to version 2.6.11.
> Uncordon the node after upgrade.
> Verify that all Milvus pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Milvus'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Milvus service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Milvus pods with 'kubectl get pods -n milvus' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources available.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust resource requests in the Milvus Helm chart.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the Helm chart version or configuration.

**Fix:**
Check the Helm release status with 'helm status milvus --namespace milvus' and review the configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Milvus pod may be crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n milvus' to diagnose the issue.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new vector indexing algorithms and improved query performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old indexing methods in favor of the new ones.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.4, ensure to migrate your indexing configurations to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see the Milvus pods running with the output of 'kubectl get pods -n milvus' showing their status as 'Running'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster and the necessary tools installed.
